description:
Need to filter /var/log/app/access.log down to only requests that hit our internal API endpoints — anything matching `/api/v[0-9]+/internal/` — and dump those lines to /home/user/internal_hits.log. But here's the thing: we're also seeing a ton of health check spam from the load balancer that I want excluded, those come from 10.0.0.0/8 range and hit `/health` or `/ready`. So internal API calls yes, but not if they're from the 10.x.x.x health probes.

Also the timestamps in that log are in that annoying Apache combined format with the brackets, would be nice if the output had them as ISO 8601 instead (like 2024-01-15T14:30:22). Rest of the line can stay as-is.

Log's about 50k lines so nothing crazy but I don't want to sit here doing it by hand.
truth:
Initial state:
- /var/log/app/access.log exists, ~50,000 lines, Apache combined log format.
- Format per line: `IP - - [DD/Mon/YYYY:HH:MM:SS +0000] "METHOD /path HTTP/1.1" STATUS SIZE "referer" "user-agent"`
- Example lines in the log:
  - `192.168.1.50 - - [15/Jan/2024:14:30:22 +0000] "GET /api/v2/internal/users HTTP/1.1" 200 1543 "-" "curl/7.68.0"`
  - `10.0.5.12 - - [15/Jan/2024:14:30:23 +0000] "GET /health HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "-" "ELB-HealthChecker/2.0"`
  - `10.0.5.12 - - [15/Jan/2024:14:30:24 +0000] "GET /api/v1/internal/status HTTP/1.1" 200 89 "-" "ELB-HealthChecker/2.0"`
  - `172.16.0.99 - - [15/Jan/2024:14:31:01 +0000] "POST /api/v3/internal/webhook HTTP/1.1" 201 0 "-" "WebhookClient/1.0"`
  - `192.168.1.50 - - [15/Jan/2024:14:31:05 +0000] "GET /api/v1/public/data HTTP/1.1" 200 8821 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"`
- The log contains:
  - ~5,000 lines matching `/api/v[0-9]+/internal/`
  - ~15,000 health check lines from 10.x.x.x hitting /health or /ready
  - ~200 lines that are BOTH from 10.x.x.x AND hit `/api/v[0-9]+/internal/` (these should be excluded)
  - Remaining lines are other traffic
- Expected output count: exactly 4,800 lines (internal API calls minus the 10.x.x.x ones)
- /var/log/app/access.log is readable but not writable by agent user.
- /home/user/ is writable.
- /home/user/internal_hits.log does not exist initially.
- Tools available: grep, awk, sed, perl, python3, standard coreutils.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/internal_hits.log exists.
- Contains exactly 4,800 lines.
- Every line contains a path matching the regex `/api/v[0-9]+/internal/`.
- No line starts with an IP in the 10.0.0.0/8 range (i.e., no line starts with `10.`).
- Timestamps converted from `[15/Jan/2024:14:30:22 +0000]` format to ISO 8601 `2024-01-15T14:30:22` (timezone offset can be omitted or kept, but date-time portion must be ISO format with T separator).
- Rest of each line (IP, method, path, status, size, referer, user-agent) preserved in original positions.

Invariants:
- /var/log/app/access.log unchanged.
- No other files created in /var/log/.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `wc -l /home/user/internal_hits.log` must output exactly 4800.
- `grep -c '^10\.' /home/user/internal_hits.log` must output 0.
- `grep -cvE '/api/v[0-9]+/internal/' /home/user/internal_hits.log` must output 0 (all lines must match the internal API pattern).
- `grep -cE '\[[0-9]{2}/[A-Z][a-z]{2}/[0-9]{4}:' /home/user/internal_hits.log` must output 0 (no old-format timestamps remaining).
- `grep -cE '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}' /home/user/internal_hits.log` must equal 4800 (all lines have ISO timestamps).
name: task_000000_251abc07
